sentences.sentence: Most countries' education systems have had what you might call educational disasters, but, sadly, in many areas of certain countries these 'disasters' are still evident today.
sentences.translation: 大多数国家的教育体系都经历过可称为教育灾难的问题；但遗憾的是，在某些国家的许多地区，这些“灾难”至今仍清晰可见。

structure.text: Most countries' education systems have had what you might call educational disasters
what you might call educational disasters
but, sadly, in many areas of certain countries these 'disasters' are still evident today

difficult_points: what you might call ... 是融合关系从句，作 had 的宾语，意为“你或许会称作……的事物”。
but 后的 sadly 是评注性插入语；引号表示 disasters 是带评价色彩的说法。

sentences.sentence: The English education system is unique due to the fact that there are still dozens of schools which are known as private schools and they perpetuate privilege and social division.
sentences.translation: 英国教育体系的独特之处在于，至今仍有数十所被称为私立学校的学校，它们延续着特权与社会分化。

structure.text: The English education system is unique
due to the fact that there are still dozens of schools
which are known as private schools
and they perpetuate privilege and social division

difficult_points: that 引导同位语从句，解释 the fact 的具体内容。
which 从句修饰 schools，be known as 表示“被称为”。
they 指前面的 private schools，而不是 education system。

sentences.sentence: Most countries have some private schools for the children of the wealthy; England is able to more than triple the average number globally.
sentences.translation: 大多数国家都有一些供富裕家庭子女就读的私立学校；而英国的数量达到全球平均水平的三倍多。

structure.text: Most countries have some private schools for the children of the wealthy
England
is able to more than triple the average number globally

difficult_points: 分号连接两个密切相关的独立分句，后句拿英国与全球平均水平比较。
more than triple 把 triple 用作动词，表示“达到三倍以上”；表达略不自然但保留源文。

sentences.sentence: England has around 3,000 private schools and just under half a million children are educated at them whilst some nine million children are educated at state schools.
sentences.translation: 英国约有3000所私立学校，近50万名儿童在其中就读，而约900万名儿童在公立学校接受教育。

structure.text: England has around 3,000 private schools
and just under half a million children are educated at them
whilst some nine million children are educated at state schools

difficult_points: 两个 are educated 都是被动语态，at them 中 them 指 private schools。
whilst 在此表示对比，相当于 whereas；some 修饰数字时意为“大约”。

sentences.sentence: The overwhelming majority of students at private schools also come from middle-class families.
sentences.translation: 私立学校绝大多数学生也来自中产阶级家庭。

structure.text: The overwhelming majority of students at private schools
also come from middle-class families

difficult_points: 句子主语核心是 majority，of students 与 at private schools 逐层限定。
overwhelming 修饰 majority，强调比例极高。

sentences.sentence: The result of this system is evident and it has much English history embedded within it.
sentences.translation: 这一制度的结果显而易见，而且其中深深嵌入了英国的历史。

structure.text: The result of this system is evident
and it has much English history embedded within it
embedded within it

difficult_points: 第一个 it 指 this system；第二个 it 同样指该制度。
has ... embedded 是“have + 宾语 + 过去分词”结构，表示历史被嵌入其中。

sentences.sentence: The facts seem to speak for themselves.
sentences.translation: 事实似乎已经不言自明。

structure.text: The facts
seem to speak for themselves

difficult_points: seem to 表示说话者基于现象作出的判断。
speak for themselves 是拟人化固定表达，意为事实本身足以说明问题。

expressions.meaning_zh: 不言自明；事实本身说明问题

sentences.sentence: In the private system almost half the students go on to University, whilst in the state system only about eight per cent make it to further education.
sentences.translation: 在私立教育体系中，近一半学生会继续升入大学；而在公立教育体系中，只有约8%的学生能进入高等教育阶段。

structure.text: In the private system almost half the students go on to University
whilst in the state system only about eight per cent make it to further education

difficult_points: whilst 引导对比，将私立与公立体系的升学比例并置。
go on to 与 make it to 都表示成功进入下一教育阶段。

sentences.sentence: However, statistics such as these can be deceptive due to the fact that middle-class children do better at examinations than working class ones, and most of them stay on at school after 16.
sentences.translation: 然而，这类统计数据可能具有误导性，因为中产阶级子女的考试表现优于工人阶级子女，而且他们大多在16岁后继续留校学习。

structure.text: statistics such as these can be deceptive
due to the fact that middle-class children do better at examinations than working class ones
and most of them stay on at school after 16

difficult_points: that 从句解释 the fact；ones 替代 children，避免重复。
them 依语境指 middle-class children，stay on 表示超过通常节点后继续。

sentences.sentence: Private schools therefore have the advantage over state schools as they are entirely 'middle class', and this creates an environment of success where students work harder and apply themselves more diligently to their school work.
sentences.translation: 因此，私立学校比公立学校更具优势，因为其学生完全来自“中产阶级”；这营造出一种成功氛围，学生在其中更加努力，也更勤勉地投入学业。

structure.text: Private schools therefore have the advantage over state schools
as they are entirely 'middle class'
and this creates an environment of success
where students work harder and apply themselves more diligently to their school work

difficult_points: as 引导原因从句，they 指 private schools，实际强调其学生构成。
this 指前述全中产阶级环境；where 从句修饰 environment。
apply themselves to 表示专心投入某事。

sentences.sentence: Private schools are extortionately expensive, being as much as £18,000 a year at somewhere such as Harrow or Eton, where Princes William and Harry attended, and at least £8,000 a year almost everywhere else.
sentences.translation: 私立学校收费高得惊人：在哈罗或伊顿这样的学校，每年学费可高达1.8万英镑，威廉王子和哈里王子都曾在那里就读；几乎其他所有私校每年至少也要8000英镑。

structure.text: Private schools are extortionately expensive
being as much as £18,000 a year at somewhere such as Harrow or Eton
where Princes William and Harry attended
and at least £8,000 a year almost everywhere else

difficult_points: being ... 是现在分词短语，补充说明昂贵的具体程度。
where 修饰 Harrow or Eton；句末并列项省略 being。

sentences.sentence: There are many parents who are not wealthy or even comfortably off but are willing to sacrifice a great deal in the cause of their children's schooling.
sentences.translation: 许多家长并不富裕，甚至谈不上生活宽裕，却愿意为孩子的教育作出巨大牺牲。

structure.text: There are many parents
who are not wealthy or even comfortably off
but are willing to sacrifice a great deal in the cause of their children's schooling

difficult_points: who 从句修饰 parents，but 并列该从句内部的两个谓语。
comfortably off 表示经济宽裕；in the cause of 表示为了某项事业或目标。

sentences.sentence: It baffles many people as to why they need to spend such vast amounts when there are perfectly acceptable state schools that don't cost a penny.
sentences.translation: 既然有完全可以接受且分文不收的公立学校，为什么他们还要花如此巨款，这令许多人困惑。

structure.text: It baffles many people
as to why they need to spend such vast amounts
when there are perfectly acceptable state schools
that don't cost a penny

difficult_points: It 指后面 as to why 所表达的问题内容，why 从句说明困惑之处。
when 在此近似“既然、在……情况下”，不是单纯时间。
that 从句修饰 state schools，not cost a penny 强调免费。

sentences.sentence: One father gave his reasoning for sending his son to a private school, 'If my son gets a five-percent-better chance of going to University then that may be the difference between success and failure."
sentences.translation: 一位父亲解释了送儿子上私立学校的理由：“如果我儿子上大学的机会能提高5%，那或许就是成功与失败之间的差别。”

structure.text: One father gave his reasoning for sending his son to a private school
'If my son gets a five-percent-better chance of going to University
then that may be the difference between success and failure

difficult_points: for sending ... 修饰 reasoning，说明理由所针对的行为。
If ... then ... 构成条件关系，that 指提高5%的入学机会。
引语开头用单引号、结尾用双引号，是源文引号不匹配的排版异常。

expressions.text: the difference between success and failure
expressions.meaning_zh: 成功与失败之间的差别

sentences.sentence: It would seem to the average person that a £50,000 minimum total cost of second level education is a lot to pay for a five-percent-better chance.
sentences.translation: 在普通人看来，仅为增加5%的机会就为中等教育支付至少5万英镑，似乎代价太高。

structure.text: It would seem to the average person
that a £50,000 minimum total cost of second level education is a lot to pay
for a five-percent-better chance

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，that 从句是真正的主语内容。
a lot to pay 表示需要支付的代价很高，to pay 后省略宾语。
five-percent-better 是源文带连字符的复合比较修饰语。

sentences.sentence: Most children, given the choice, would take the money and spend it on more enjoyable things rather than shelling it out on a school that is too posh for its own good
sentences.translation: 如果可以选择，大多数孩子都会拿这笔钱去买更有趣的东西，而不是把钱花在一所高档得有些过头的学校上。

structure.text: Most children
given the choice
would take the money and spend it on more enjoyable things
rather than shelling it out on a school that is too posh for its own good

difficult_points: given the choice 是过去分词独立性状语，意为“如果让他们选择”。
shell it out on 表示把钱大量花在某物上，it 指 money。
that 修饰 school；源句末没有句号，按原文保留。

sentences.sentence: However, some say that the real reason that parents fork out the cash is prejudice: they don't want their little kids mixing with the "workers", or picking up an undesirable accent.
sentences.translation: 然而，有人说家长愿意掏钱的真正原因是偏见：他们不希望自己的孩子与“工人阶层”混在一起，也不希望孩子沾上他们不喜欢的口音。

structure.text: some say
that the real reason that parents fork out the cash is prejudice
that parents fork out the cash
they don't want their little kids mixing with the "workers", or picking up an undesirable accent

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导 say 的宾语从句，第二个 that 修饰 reason。
冒号后解释 prejudice；want + 宾语 + doing 表示不希望孩子处于这些行为中。
mixing 与 picking up 并列，their little kids 是共同逻辑主语。

sentences.sentence: In addition to this, it wouldn't do if at the next dinner party all the guests were boasting about sending their kids to the same place where the son of the third cousin of Prince Charles is going, and you say your kid is going to the state school down the road, even if you could pocket the money for yourself instead, and, as a result, be able to serve the best Champagne with the smoked salmon and duck.
sentences.translation: 此外，如果下次宴会上所有客人都在炫耀把孩子送到查尔斯王子三表亲的儿子所就读的学校，而你却说自己的孩子只上附近的公立学校，那可不太体面——即使这样你能把钱省给自己，并因此用最好的香槟搭配烟熏三文鱼和鸭肉招待客人。

structure.text: it wouldn't do
if at the next dinner party all the guests were boasting about sending their kids to the same place
where the son of the third cousin of Prince Charles is going
and you say your kid is going to the state school down the road

difficult_points: it wouldn't do 是固定评价句式，表示某种情形不合适或不体面。
where 从句修饰 the same place；say 后省略 that。
even if 引导让步从句，其中 pocket 与 be able to serve 共享主语 you。

sentences.sentence: It is a fact, however, that at many of the best private schools, your money buys you something.
sentences.translation: 不过，事实是，在许多顶尖私立学校里，你花的钱确实能买到一些实在的东西。

structure.text: It is a fact
however
that at many of the best private schools, your money buys you something

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，that 从句说明 fact 的具体内容。
buy you something 是双宾语结构，语义是金钱为你换来某些资源。

sentences.sentence: One school, with 500 pupils, has 11 science laboratories; another school with 800 pupils, has 30 music practice rooms; another has 16 squash courts, and yet another has its own beach.
sentences.translation: 一所有500名学生的学校拥有11间实验室；另一所有800名学生的学校有30间音乐练习室；还有一所有16个壁球场，另有一所拥有自己的海滩。

structure.text: One school, with 500 pupils, has 11 science laboratories
another school with 800 pupils, has 30 music practice rooms
another has 16 squash courts
and yet another has its own beach

difficult_points: 分号并列四所学校，another 与 yet another 均省略 school。
第二个主谓之间的逗号是源文多余标点，按原文保留。

sentences.sentence: Private schools spend £300 per pupil a year on investment in buildings and facilities; the state system spends less than £50.
sentences.translation: 私立学校每年为每名学生在建筑和设施投资上花费300英镑；公立体系支出不足50英镑。

structure.text: Private schools spend £300 per pupil a year on investment in buildings and facilities
the state system spends less than £50

difficult_points: per pupil a year 同时说明人均和年度两个计量维度。
分号后省略与前句相同的支出用途。

sentences.sentence: On books, the ratio is 3 to 1.
sentences.translation: 在图书支出方面，两者比例是3比1。

structure.text: On books
the ratio is 3 to 1

difficult_points: On books 是话题状语，承接上一句的支出比较。
3 to 1 的双方需由上文推断为私立与公立体系。

sentences.sentence: One of the things that your money buys which is difficult to quantify is the appearance of the school, the way it looks.
sentences.translation: 你的钱所买到、却难以量化的一项东西，是学校的外观，也就是它看起来的样子。

structure.text: One of the things that your money buys which is difficult to quantify
that your money buys
which is difficult to quantify
is the appearance of the school, the way it looks

difficult_points: that 与 which 两个定语从句都限定 things。
the way it looks 是 appearance 的同位解释，省略关系副词。

sentences.sentence: Most private schools that you will find are set in beautiful, well-kept country houses, with extensive grounds and gardens.
sentences.translation: 你所见到的大多数私立学校都坐落在漂亮且维护良好的乡间宅邸中，并拥有宽广的场地和花园。

structure.text: Most private schools that you will find
that you will find
are set in beautiful, well-kept country houses
with extensive grounds and gardens

difficult_points: that you will find 修饰 schools，带有泛指“你通常会见到的”意味。
be set in 表示坐落于；with 短语补充其附属环境。

sentences.sentence: In comparison with the state schools, they tend to look like castles, with the worst of the state schools looking like public lavatories, perhaps even tiled or covered in graffiti.
sentences.translation: 与公立学校相比，私立学校往往看起来像城堡；而最差的公立学校则像公共厕所，甚至可能铺着瓷砖或满是涂鸦。

structure.text: In comparison with the state schools
they tend to look like castles
with the worst of the state schools looking like public lavatories
perhaps even tiled or covered in graffiti

difficult_points: they 指 private schools；with 复合结构的逻辑主语是 the worst of the state schools。
tiled 与 covered 是并列过去分词，补充描述公立学校外观。

sentences.sentence: Many may even have an architectural design that is just about on the level of an industrial shed.
sentences.translation: 许多公立学校的建筑设计甚至可能仅仅相当于工业棚屋的水平。

structure.text: Many
may even have an architectural design
that is just about on the level of an industrial shed

difficult_points: Many 省略了由上文可知的 state schools。
that 从句修饰 architectural design；on the level of 表示处于相当水平。
